columns.extraction_plan: Search the entire document for the alphanumeric code following 'NCT'. If not found in the provided chunks, use 'Not reported'.
The PubMed ID is not explicitly provided in the document. While there are DOIs in the references, a PubMed ID (PMID) is not listed.
Extract the trial name "ARASENS Trial" from the title of the article on page 1.
Extract the full list of authors from the top of page 1.

Extract the year "2023" from the copyright information at the bottom of page 1 ("© 2023 by American Society of Clinical Oncology") or from the publication date in the abstract section ("published at ascopubs.org/journal/jco on February 16, 2023").
Based on the presence of 'abstract', 'INTRODUCTION', and 'METHODS' sections in Chunk 8, infer that the document is a full publication.
Extract "III" from the title of the article on page 1 ("Phase III ARASENS Trial") or from the abstract on page 1 ("Phase III trial").

Based on the abstract and introduction describing the study as presenting 'efﬁcacy and safety outcomes from ARASENS in patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer by disease volume and disease risk' and stating 'We conducted these subgroup analyses posthoc', identify it as an 'Original' report of this specific subgroup analysis.
